MINI MINUTES:

DEFENDANT UNITED SERVICES AUTOMOBILE ASSOCIATION MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE.

Grant, no opposition filed.

Order is signed.

MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS
Off Calendar Apr-03-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Damian Oksenendler, Esq. 415-733-1050 Oksenendler Law for Plaintiff.
James Hockel, Esq. 415-646-4704 Maynard Nexsen for Defendant USAA. (In-person)

ARGUED. Defendant's counsel Mr. Hockel contests the tentative ruling and presents arguments on notice to consumer and sanctions. Plaintiff's counsel Mr. Damian Oksenendler responds.
The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Plaintiffs Michael Ely and Allyson Ely's motion to quash subpoena to David Smith is Taken Under Submission. ***

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

***After the hearing the court signs an order quashing subpoena. (see scanned order for details)***

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel
Off Calendar Jul-25-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: DEFENDANT UNITED SERVICES AUTOMOBILE ASSOCIATION'S Motion To Compel.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
JAMES J. HOCKEL, Esq., of MAYNARD NEXSEN LLP, (415) 646-4700, for Defendant.
No other appearances.

Not argued/contested. The court adopts its tentative ruling. The unopposed motion of Defendant United Services Automobile Association is granted. Plaintiffs shall provide code-compliant verified responses without objections to Defendant's First Supplemental Interrogatories and First Supplemental Requests for Production of Documents within 14 days of service of notice of entry of this order. The Court also notes that this motion improperly combined two motions and violates the filing fee requirements of Govt. C. Sec. 70617(a)(f).

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem to williamclynn@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: William Lynn. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (302/JPT)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-03-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (through Zoom):
Demian Oksenendler, Esq. 415-733-1050 demian@mlolawyers.com of Mannion Lowe & Oksenendler for Plaintiff.
Nicholas Boos, Esq. 415-646-4674 nboos@maynardcooper.com for Defendant UNITED SERVICES AUTOMOBILE ASSOCIATION.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant submits on the tentative ruling on causes of action 1-4 and contests the fifth cause of action for estoppel. Plaintiff responds and argues the eight cause of action on bad faith.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT UNITED SERVICES AUTOMOBILE ASSOCIATION's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Taken Under Submission.**(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

**After the hearing, an Order REGARDING DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is signed/filed. (see scanned order for details)**
